think_blocks:
The user wants me to segment the video into 7 specific time ranges and provide a transcript and summary for each.

**Segment 1: 00:00 - 01:39**
- **Content:** Jimmy Fallon welcomes Lee Jung-jae to the show. They discuss his recent Emmy win for *Squid Game*. Fallon shows a picture of Lee holding the Emmy. Lee expresses his gratitude and how surreal the experience was, especially since the director also won.
- **Transcript:** "Thank you for being here. It's so nice to meet you in person. Last time I had you on, we Zoomed. Yes, right, two or three years ago. Yes. And it was right before Squid Game came out. It was... It's the biggest thing ever. I mean, now it's the Netflix most-watched series ever. Bigger than Stranger Things anything. I mean, you're phenomenal in it. I think I told you then, but you just acted so well. And you actually made me sad. You made me, like, love you. I felt so bad for you. I'm like, I want this guy to win the whole thing. Whatever you want to do, win the Squid Game, please. I love you. And then, yeah, I don't want to ruin anything, but if you watch it, it's just a phenomenal performance. And you won an Emmy for it, so congratulations. Well-deserved. Thank you. That night, because director Hwang and I both won, it felt twice as surreal. Even now, just thinking about it, my heart is about to burst, it's hard to put into words. It was a truly amazing night. It was unimaginable. Where do you keep your Emmy? in the middle of my living room. That's where I would keep it, too, if I got one."
- **Summary:** Jimmy Fallon welcomes Lee Jung-jae, congratulating him on his Emmy win for *Squid Game*. Lee shares his overwhelming emotions about the night, noting the surreal feeling of winning alongside the director.

**Segment 2: 01:39 - 03:13**
- **Content:** Fallon asks about Lee's career beginnings. Lee explains he was preparing for an interior design job and working at a cafe when he was suggested for a modeling gig. He took it because it paid more, which led to acting. Fallon shows a vintage photo of Lee modeling for a chocolate bar called "Crunky" and they both eat the chocolate bars.
- **Transcript:** "It's a global phenomenon, but you've had a huge career for 30 years. How did you start acting? Honestly, I was getting ready to work for an interior design company back then, and then I was getting ready to work for an interior design company back then, and then I got a part-time job working at a cafe, And I was suggested a part-time modeling gig, and it turned out, it paid a lot more than what the cafe paid me, so I thought I'd take it up. And that modeling gig led to me being cast as an actor. And I thought I'd save just a little more and quit. I didn't know that that would be the beginning to this long of a career, never knew that I'd be in something like \"Squid Game,\" and never knew I'd be here sitting with you at \"The Tonight Show.\" It's an unbelievable night. I'm so happy you're here. We have a photo of your early modeling career. This is you for a crunky chocolate bar. Look at you here. Yeah. That's, wow. It's dead. Mr. Cool. Oh, my gosh. Crunky chocolate bar. Oh, my... This is the face. I can't believe I'm meeting the man behind Crunky. It's unbelievable. Thank you. Actually, I have two crunky bars. Oh. If you want to try to recreate this scene... Yes. Evet. Wow. Very good. I like Crunky. Yeah, it's good stuff. Um. Thank you again. Sure, I can take your crunkie. Thank you very much."
- **Summary:** Lee Jung-jae recounts his accidental entry into acting via a modeling gig for a chocolate bar, which paid better than his cafe job. Fallon humorously displays a vintage ad and they both sample the chocolate bars.

**Segment 3: 03:13 - 03:53**
- **Content:** They discuss the release of *Squid Game* Season 2 on December 26th. Lee mentions he will be catching up on sleep on Christmas Day after his press tour and then binge-watching the new season with everyone.
- **Transcript:** "Thank you again for being here. That's the new season of Squid Game, December 26th, the day after Christmas. That's right. How will you be celebrating Christmas? uh, uh, uh, We've been in L.A., now in New York for the press tour, So, I think on the 25th, I'm going to need to catch up on my sleep,"
- **Summary:** Fallon confirms the release date for Season 2, and Lee jokes that his Christmas celebration will involve sleeping after his press tour before binge-watching the new episodes.

**Segment 4: 03:53 - 05:48**
- **Content:** Lee talks about the pressure of following up Season 1 but assures fans Season 2 is well-made. They play a game where Lee answers fan questions using "Yes" and "No" buzzers. He confirms the doll returns, he is the only winner returning, he is not the new Front Man, there are new games, and Season 3 is coming in 2025.
- **Transcript:** "and on the 26th, I'll be binge watching season 2 with all of you. Were you? Were you hesitant to do season two? Was there pressure to do season two? Because season 1 was so widely loved, we did feel the pressure about whether or not season 2 would be just as loved. However, our story has gotten more diverse, deeper, with a very clear theme. So I don't know if I can say it will be as successful, but what I can say, is that it's just as well made. Ooh. How do we set up what's happening in Season 2? First of all, many people are going to get killed. Yeah, I thought that. I thought that was gonna happen, yeah. And I'm going to do my best to stay alive. Yeah. Fans have a lot of questions about this new season of Squid Game, and I wanted to see if you could answer some, okay? Now, I have these buttons right here, okay? Uh-oh. Okay? If it is no... Okay? Okay, we all remember... Don't Will she be back in season two? Yes, she will! I love this game. I love this. I love you. We know that you're back in the game this time. Will you be competing against other people who've also won the game? No! He's really the only winner of the game. This is so fun. This is so fun. Do you become the new front man? That's the rumor. And are you now in charge of the games? Whoa! Really? I think the internet just broke right there. Wow! Okay, will we see any new games in Season 2? Okay, good. Hopefully, yeah. I'll be out. Is Season 3 of Squid Game coming to Netflix in 2025? You Season three. Yes. Thank you so much for playing this. I want to show everyone a clip. Here's Lee Jung Jae in the new season of Squid Game. Take a look."
- **Summary:** Lee discusses the pressure of Season 2's production and teases the plot. He then plays a buzzer game, confirming the return of the doll, his status as the sole returning winner, the absence of him as the Front Man, new games, and the confirmation of Season 3 in 2025.

**Segment 5: 05:48 - 06:45**
- **Content:** A clip from *Squid Game* Season 2 plays. Lee's character, Gi-hun, is talking to another player (Player 218) about why he returned to the game instead of living a new life with the money.
- **Transcript:** "So why did you come back? If I were you, I would have put everything behind me and started a new life with the money. If... I died right before your eyes, and you... won all the money, how would you feel? Come on, don't say that. We'll make it out together. That's why I came back. To make it out with everyone. To end this game. But I don't know anymore."
- **Summary:** A dramatic clip from Season 2 shows Gi-hun explaining his motivation for returning to the game: to end it and save everyone, rather than just surviving for himself.

**Segment 6: 06:45 - 07:01**
- **Content:** The clip continues with Gi-hun looking conflicted and the other player looking hopeful but uncertain.
- **Transcript:** (No speech, just visual continuation of the scene)
- **Summary:** The scene concludes with Gi-hun expressing uncertainty about his plan to end the game, highlighting the emotional weight of his decision.

**Segment 7: 07:01 - 07:59**
- **Content:** Back in the studio, Fallon thanks Lee and promotes the show's release date again. The video ends with an outro screen.
- **Transcript:** "Lee Jung-jae, everybody! Squid Game Season 2 premieres December 26 on Netflix. More tonight's show after the break. Stick around, everybody."
- **Summary:** Fallon wraps up the interview, thanking Lee Jung-jae and reminding viewers of the Season 2 premiere date on Netflix.
